domain_focus: DocRED-style document-level relation extraction over entity clusters. Use the dataset entities, entity types, sentence IDs, relation labels and the fixed DocRED ontology as the main constraints. The task is not open information extraction; it is constrained relation prediction between known document entities.
abstraction_level: entity-cluster level. Prefer canonical document entities over surface mentions. Keep relation predictions grounded in document sentences and relation directions.
population_policy: Use only relation labels allowed by the DocRED ontology/profile. Do not invent new relation names. Do not create a relation from co-occurrence alone. Prefer high-precision predictions. A relation can be document-level and cross-sentence, but it must be supported by explicit wording, apposition, membership, part-of statements, location statements, or a strong document-level bridge. Preserve the head->tail direction exactly as defined by the relation label.
event_modeling_preference: Although internal NeoOLAF layers may use event/candidate terminology, DocRED should be treated as entity-centric relation extraction. Entities such as ORG, LOC, TIME and MISC are candidate nodes; relations connect entity clusters, not newly invented events.

typing_examples.explanation: A television network or media organization should be typed as ORG.
A media group/company should be typed as ORG.
A city/location should be typed as LOC.
A country should be typed as LOC.
A date expression should be typed as TIME.
A language/nationality adjective can be MISC when the dataset entity type says so; do not automatically convert it into a country entity.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV is a Greek free-to-air television network based in Piraeus.
relation_examples.explanation: The expression 'based in Piraeus' supports an ORG -> LOC headquarters/location relation in the DocRED profile.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV is a Greek free-to-air television network based in Piraeus.
relation_examples.explanation: The adjective 'Greek' and the document context support the country relation to Greece when Greece is an entity candidate.

relation_examples.text: It is part of the Skai Group, one of the largest media groups in the country.
relation_examples.explanation: The phrase 'part of the Skai Group' is accepted as ownership/parent-group evidence under the DocRED relation label.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV is also a member of Digea, a consortium of private television networks introducing digital terrestrial transmission in Greece.
relation_examples.explanation: The appositive and location phrase place Digea in Greece.

relation_examples.text: It was relaunched in its present form on 1st of April 2006 in the Athens metropolitan area.
relation_examples.explanation: DocRED examples may accept document-level location evidence even when the surface phrase is not a strict headquarters phrase; follow the dataset ontology/examples.

promotion_examples.explanation: A DocRED/Wikidata property label should be promoted as an ontology relation.
A DocRED/Wikidata property label should be promoted as an ontology relation with ORG-like domain and LOC-like range when applicable.
Dataset entities should remain document-level instances/candidates unless the ontology explicitly requires concept promotion.

negative_examples.text: Skai TV is available on the subscription-based encrypted services of Nova and Cosmote TV.
negative_examples.explanation: Availability on a service/platform does not imply owned by, country, headquarters location, or membership unless the allowed relation explicitly matches it.

negative_examples.text: Skai TV opted for dubbing all foreign language content into Greek instead of using subtitles.
negative_examples.explanation: The language 'Greek' in content/dubbing context should not create a country relation by itself.

negative_examples.text: Skai TV and Digea appear in the same sentence.
negative_examples.explanation: Co-occurrence alone is not sufficient for a relation. The phrase 'member of' may support a membership relation only if that relation is allowed by the ontology/profile.

negative_examples.text: Piraeus and Athens are both locations mentioned in the document.
negative_examples.explanation: Do not create a relation between two locations unless the relation is explicitly supported and allowed. For P17, the country target should be Greece, not another city.
